best_used_as: Primary scorer in half-court sets; featured in late-clock and transition situations
avoid_using_as: Defensive anchor or primary rebounder in coverage schemes
lineup_fit_note: Best next to a true floor spacer or secondary creator to prevent defensive loading
primary_caution: Efficiency sustains under current usage; extending role creation responsibilities shifts that balance
coaching_takeaway_short: Deploy as the featured scoring option; protect by limiting play-creation duties outside designed sets.
coaching_takeaway_long: Her combination of shot volume and efficiency makes her a reliable primary option in structured half-court offenses. Keep creation duties within designed actions — unstructured on-ball reps risk both turnovers and shot quality. Pair with a secondary playmaker to maintain spacing and reduce defensive collapse.

best_used_as: Secondary shot creator or change-of-pace scorer in transition situations
avoid_using_as: Primary isolation option in late-game half-court sets; featured in any play requiring shot quality under pressure
lineup_fit_note: Works best next to an efficient primary scorer who can draw the defense before she receives the ball
primary_caution: High volume without matching efficiency inflates possession cost; usage should contract in tight game situations
coaching_takeaway_short: Use in scripted actions and transition bursts; limit high-volume isolation reps where shot quality degrades.
coaching_takeaway_long: She generates volume but not sustainable efficiency at peak usage levels. Structured touches in transition or from movement are the highest-value deployment. In half-court, design actions that create open looks rather than off-the-dribble creation opportunities. Watch possession cost in late-game situations.

best_used_as: Secondary creator in ball-movement offenses; off-ball shooter and read-game connector
avoid_using_as: Primary ball-handler or lead initiator under full-possession creation pressure
lineup_fit_note: Scales well beside a high-usage primary option who draws pressure and creates open reads
primary_caution: Efficiency has not been tested at higher usage; pushing her into primary creation without structure may expose limitations
coaching_takeaway_short: Use as a creation layer behind your primary option; let efficiency and decision-making do the work rather than volume.
coaching_takeaway_long: Her shot quality and ball security are elite at current usage levels. The risk is over-extending on-ball responsibilities before testing that ceiling in structure. Start by adding one more designed touch per half before committing to expanded role. Her comp profile suggests real primary upside — but controlled.

primary_risk: Limited scoring threat if spacing is absent
best_used_as: Ball-movement connector and floor spacer in continuity offenses
avoid_using_as: Primary shot creator or late-clock scoring option
lineup_fit_note: Best in lineups with at least two reliable scorers she can connect to; works in any formation with secondary creation needs
primary_caution: Scoring output is low; if spacing collapses and she becomes the primary focus, her value diminishes quickly
coaching_takeaway_short: She is a system contributor whose value is organizational — use her to maintain spacing, connect actions, and limit turnovers.
coaching_takeaway_long: Her game lives in the margins: clean decisions, limited turnovers, and smart floor positioning. She is not a solution to scoring problems, but she makes a scoring lineup better by keeping plays in motion. Limit high-stakes creation asks and let her play within the flow.

primary_risk: Limited creation if primary scorers are contained
best_used_as: Off-ball shooter in catch-and-shoot actions; gravity piece in spread formations
avoid_using_as: Ball-handler or on-ball creator in any structured half-court set
lineup_fit_note: Needs a driving primary guard or post threat to draw help before she gets the ball
primary_caution: Pull-up creation and shot creation off movement are limited; if defense goes under screens, she must demonstrate range consistency
coaching_takeaway_short: She is a pure spacing threat — build actions that get her the ball in rhythm and off the catch, not off the dribble.
coaching_takeaway_long: Her value is floor impact, not ball-handling value. Place her in spacial positions where the primary action draws help and leaves her open. Avoid play designs that require her to create off the bounce. In the right lineup, she converts at a high rate and opens the lane for others.

best_used_as: Defensive versatility piece and secondary scorer in transitional lineups
avoid_using_as: Pure floor spacer or primary ball-security anchor; she needs defensive action to maximize value
lineup_fit_note: Best beside ball-dominant guards who benefit from a defensive disruptor and secondary scoring option
primary_caution: Extended creation responsibility elevates turnover exposure; limit off-the-dribble creation in half-court
coaching_takeaway_short: Feature her defensive versatility and use scoring as the secondary layer; do not overload with ball-handling pressure.
coaching_takeaway_long: She produces at the defensive end and can score in transition and secondary creation contexts. The risk is treating her like a full primary creator — that exposure lifts her turnover rate. Deployment should lean on her defensive utility first, with structured offensive touches layered in.

primary_risk: Spacing stress if perimeter is underdeveloped
best_used_as: Interior scoring hub in high-post and short-roll actions; primary finisher in dribble-drive systems
avoid_using_as: Three-point spacing option or switch target in extended pick-and-roll coverage
lineup_fit_note: Needs shooters on the perimeter to open the lane; paired with a distributing guard she becomes the offense's anchor
primary_caution: If the floor is not spaced, her efficiency drops as the lane closes; perimeter spacing quality directly affects output
coaching_takeaway_short: Build the offense through her touch near the basket — surround with shooters and she is your most efficient option.
coaching_takeaway_long: She is most dangerous as a near-basket option in structured sets where perimeter shooting keeps the lane open. Her efficiency in those conditions is elite. Prioritize spacing in starting lineup construction and let her interior presence be the primary creation threat. Defense will have to choose between collapsing and abandoning shooters.

difference_dimension: Defensive Anchor Value vs Offensive Limitation
best_used_as: Defensive anchor and rebounding base; interior presence in drop-coverage schemes
avoid_using_as: Primary ball-handler or featured scoring option in isolation or high-post creation sets
lineup_fit_note: Scales beside guards with high creation volume; lets perimeter players hunt the rim behind her screens
primary_caution: Will not solve scoring deficits; overusing in scoring situations removes defensive value without adding offensive return
coaching_takeaway_short: Deploy as your defensive foundation and rebounding floor — build scoring layers around her, not through her.
coaching_takeaway_long: Her value is organizational on the defensive end: protecting the rim and cleaning the glass. She is a reliable partner for perimeter-creation guards who need a finishing layer and help-side anchor. Do not force scoring responsibilities — let her play to her structural strengths and add scoring only through opportunistic putbacks and short-roll finishes.

best_used_as: Primary interior threat in structured post and short-roll sets; defensive anchor in half-court
avoid_using_as: Ball-handler in press break or extended high-low reads requiring multiple dribble touches
lineup_fit_note: Needs a distributing guard or playmaking forward to absorb pressure and handle ball-screen decisions
primary_caution: Turnover rate is elevated; ball-screen actions that place her in reads she cannot reset cleanly increase possession cost
coaching_takeaway_short: Limit her to catch-and-finish reads; do not place her in ball-handling situations that expose turnover tendency.
coaching_takeaway_long: She produces dominant interior value — the caution is not her scoring, it is the ball-handling load. Keep decisions simple near the basket and avoid complex actions requiring multiple reads in space. A playmaking partner at guard takes pressure off and allows her interior game to shine without the turnover cost.

primary_risk: None identified at current deployment level
difference_dimension: Two-Way Ceiling vs Single-End Specialist Peers
best_used_as: Primary option in any offensive set; defensive assignment on opposing best perimeter player
avoid_using_as: Off-ball spacer role — she is wasted in systems that minimize on-ball creation opportunities
lineup_fit_note: Does not require spacing to succeed; works in any formation but unlocks most value next to a secondary creator who can draw pressure
primary_caution: Profile is rare; the primary caution is deployment — do not limit her to a single-end role when both ends of the floor are available to her
coaching_takeaway_short: Give her the ball and the defensive assignment — she is a system-elevating player who should have maximum flexibility on both ends.
coaching_takeaway_long: This is your franchise piece. She produces at the highest level on both ends and handles pressure without turnover exposure. Let her run scripted actions and switch freely on defense. The only misuse is restriction — she should have the most freedom in your system, not the least.

difference_dimension: Usage Volume vs Shot Quality Consistency
best_used_as: Lead on-ball scorer in transition and secondary creation sets; defensive versatility piece
avoid_using_as: Sole scoring option in structured half-court execution where efficiency consistency is required
lineup_fit_note: Best next to a reliable efficient scorer who stabilizes scoring output when she has a quiet shooting night
primary_caution: Scoring volatility means some games she is dominant and others she struggles to convert — game-plan must account for off-nights
coaching_takeaway_short: She provides the scoring engine and defensive energy; manage shot-quality variance by distributing scoring responsibilities.
coaching_takeaway_long: Her on/off scoring swings are the key variable. When she is on, she can take over a game — when she is not converting, possession cost climbs. Building a second reliable scoring option in the rotation covers her variance and makes your team harder to defend. Ball security is not the concern; shot selection and conversion are.

best_used_as: Catch-and-shoot specialist and secondary creator in perimeter-heavy sets; primary spacing option in drive-heavy systems
avoid_using_as: Defensive anchor or help-side rim protector; do not give her rotation-heavy defensive assignments
lineup_fit_note: Best next to a dominant on-ball guard or high-post center who creates open looks through pressure
primary_caution: Defensive exposure in help rotations; assigning her switch-heavy duties or rim coverage risks defensive breakdowns
coaching_takeaway_short: She is one of your best offensive weapons — protect her by keeping defensive assignments within her range.
coaching_takeaway_long: When she is shooting well, she stretches the floor and opens lanes for everyone else. The coaching challenge is coverage design — she should not be placed in situations that require extended range defense or rotation into the paint. Keep her on perimeter assignments and let the offense reward you for it.

best_used_as: Spacing role player and spot-up shooter in scripted actions
avoid_using_as: Primary scorer or late-clock option; do not isolate her when the game is on the line
lineup_fit_note: Best when surrounded by creation-heavy players; works in any system but should stay on the perimeter
primary_caution: Inconsistent scoring nights can become a momentum issue; have a contingency if her shot is cold
coaching_takeaway_short: She adds floor spacing and baseline consistency; build her into actions, not as the primary option.
coaching_takeaway_long: Her role is supplementary but meaningful — spacing contributions and consistent execution within scripted actions. The risk is overextending her into scoring situations where she becomes a liability. Use as the fourth or fifth scoring option in a designed flow, not as a primary rotation piece.

best_used_as: Scripted primary scorer in half-court sets with clear reads and limited improvisation
avoid_using_as: Lead initiator in live-ball, transition-heavy possessions or late-clock isolation reads requiring complex decisions
lineup_fit_note: Needs a ball-secure secondary handler to absorb off-the-bounce pressure when sets break down
primary_caution: Turnover rate climbs under unstructured usage; possession efficiency suffers when she is forced to improvise
coaching_takeaway_short: Keep her in designed reads — she scores at an elite level in structure but loses the ball when forced outside of it.
coaching_takeaway_long: She is one of your best scorers in any designed action. The caution is what happens when plays break down — live-ball creation exposes her ball security. Build sets that end in catches or clear drive lanes rather than dribble-heavy improvisation. A ball-security-first backup handler extends her value by cleaning up possession errors.

best_used_as: Change-of-pace scorer and momentum creator; featured in transition and quick-read situations
avoid_using_as: Sole scoring option in controlled late-game situations requiring consistent production
lineup_fit_note: Pair with a consistent, efficiency-driven scorer to stabilize output across games
primary_caution: Scoring swings are real — high-variance games create planning challenges; do not design around her as the only scoring anchor
coaching_takeaway_short: Plan for both her ceiling and her floor — build a second reliable scoring lane so variance does not become a loss driver.
coaching_takeaway_long: She can take over any game when she is on — but tournament projection and tight-game reliance must account for her variance. Create a complementary scoring option who thrives in exactly the possessions she struggles with: structured, off-movement, late-game execution. The pairing covers the downside and still benefits from her upside.

best_used_as: Facilitator and secondary creator; featured in actions that lead to others, not to her own shot
avoid_using_as: Primary shooter or featured scoring option in any late-game situation
lineup_fit_note: Best when the lineup does not depend on her scoring; let her connect, not convert
primary_caution: Shot selection should contract — reducing volume improves team efficiency faster than improving conversion rate
coaching_takeaway_short: Limit shooting volume and maximize playmaking reps — her value is in creation, not conversion.
coaching_takeaway_long: She contributes through ball movement, decision-making, and floor positioning — not through the shot itself. Trying to fix her efficiency through more attempts does not work; reduce her shooting responsibility and route plays through her facilitate-and-kick actions instead. Pair with a high-conversion scorer who benefits from her distributing.

difference_dimension: Interior Scoring vs Free Throw Pressure Threat
best_used_as: Interior finisher in high-percentage situations; post scorer in actions that generate close-range looks
avoid_using_as: Foul-hunting initiator or late-game intentional-foul situation player in a close game
lineup_fit_note: Should not be the primary hack-a target option; design actions around her where contact can be avoided or turned into easier finishes
primary_caution: Opponents may play her for fouls in late-game situations; have a substitution or action plan ready when the game goes to foul strategy
coaching_takeaway_short: Feature her interior scoring but have a late-game plan when opponents shift to intentional-foul strategy.
coaching_takeaway_long: She is a reliable interior producer in the flow of the game. The liability is foul management — opponents who know her free-throw rate will target her in the last two minutes. Have a clear substitution plan and set of actions that keep the ball out of her hands in late foul situations while protecting her interior output in the first 35 minutes.

best_used_as: Offensive centerpiece; off-ball in defensive sets where her guard assignment is low-threat
avoid_using_as: Primary perimeter defender or isolation stopper against opposing creators
lineup_fit_note: Must be protected defensively — pair with a switchable, length-capable defender who can take difficult assignments
primary_caution: Defensive breakdown risk is real when she is targeted in pick-and-roll coverage or assigned to opposing primary scorers
coaching_takeaway_short: Let her offense lead; design defensive rotations so she is rarely in 1-on-1 isolation against high-value offensive targets.
coaching_takeaway_long: She produces at an elite level offensively. The coaching requirement is keeping her defensive liability contained — use coverage schemes that funnel drives away from her, assign lower-threat offensive players her way, and make sure your help-side structure bails her out. She adds too much offense to sit; the solution is scheme, not the bench.

best_used_as: High-post playmaker and ball-movement hub; facilitating center in an offense that requires interior passing
avoid_using_as: Rim-protection anchor or drop coverage defender in schemes that rely on interior blocking
lineup_fit_note: Needs a weak-side shot-blocker or help-side wing to compensate for rim protection gap
primary_caution: Without interior blocking, opponents will attack the rim — defensive scheme must address this structurally, not through her alone
coaching_takeaway_short: Use her playmaking at the elbow; build rim-protection coverage around her with personnel and scheme.
coaching_takeaway_long: Her passing, decision-making, and interior facilitation make her a rare playmaking center. The defensive challenge is the rim — she is not a shot-blocker, and schemes that depend on interior deterrence will be exposed. Pair with athletic help-side players and design rotations that limit opposing interior drives. Let her offensive facilitation value lead.

best_used_as: Defensive specialist matchup piece; featured in the defensive rotation against opposing primary scorers
avoid_using_as: Primary scorer or featured ball-handler; do not extend offensive responsibilities beyond catch-and-kick actions
lineup_fit_note: Best in lineups built around two reliable scorers — her defensive value adds without requiring her to contribute offensively
primary_caution: Placing her in offensive creation situations drains what makes her valuable; keep the offense simple and the defense intense
coaching_takeaway_short: She is a defensive weapon — use her on the opposing team's best perimeter threat and keep offensive asks minimal.
coaching_takeaway_long: Her entire value is on the defensive end. The worst use is asking her to carry offense — it dilutes her impact without gaining anything. Design your rotation so she replaces a weaker defender, not a weaker scorer. When she is on the floor, your defense should get better; your offense just needs to not get worse.

primary_risk: Limited creation ceiling; dependent on others to generate shots
best_used_as: Interior finisher and floor-presence player; featured in pick-and-roll finishing actions
avoid_using_as: Primary post scorer or high-isolation option — she finishes what others create, not the other way around
lineup_fit_note: Best next to a dominant on-ball guard who generates interior passes; needs creation support to unlock her efficiency
primary_caution: If the guards cannot generate looks for her, she disappears — her production is a downstream function of others' creation
coaching_takeaway_short: She maximizes every touch she gets — give her clean catch-and-finish opportunities and she will deliver.
coaching_takeaway_long: She is a near-elite finisher who converts at a high rate on limited attempts. The challenge is feeding her consistently — she cannot create her own shot at volume, so the burden falls on your guards. Systems with high dribble-penetration or post-entry passing generate more of her best opportunities. She rewards patient ball movement.

best_used_as: Sixth player or second-unit spark; change-of-pace scorer in transition and secondary creation situations
avoid_using_as: Starting primary option in controlled, late-game situations requiring sustained consistent production
lineup_fit_note: Works well as a rotation piece in lineups that need burst scoring; does not require a creation system around her
primary_caution: Her production can be spectacular or absent — do not over-rely on her as the go-to option in elimination situations
coaching_takeaway_short: She is a burst scorer — use her rotation minutes to generate energy and quick scoring, then return to your primary rotation anchor.
coaching_takeaway_long: She scores in chunks, not in steady streams. The right deployment is as the lineup's spark plug: fresh bursts, transition looks, and secondary creation reads. She is not your closing option in tight games — but she can change the momentum of a game in the second or third quarter in a way your starters cannot.
